@jojoroussy from what I’ve learned that’s true but only if you feed them raw. Because when they lay they’ll peck at them and then eat them. I personally cook them and then feed them their eggs. We’ll sprinkle in some eggshell here and there it’s a treat and has a good source of calcium. But too much egg shell because of the high calcium can also cause problems. So egg shells here and there. Hope this helps!

text: let’s see the yolks
Aghhhh 😢 I miss having my babies 🐓

Trying too hard.. feed them left over human scraps or food whatever it may be, on top of their normal diet and they will be just as healthy and produce more eggs than you can consume everyday. Even in “slow season”. Oyster shells aren’t a must, you can also use old egg shells (what ever left over you used for breakfast is fine each day). The vitamins - etc - are a must. But no need to waste buying fresh produce. You’ll do more justice (for the environment too) using your own home scraps.
